intro: Scirpus ardea, also known as Yellow Bulrush or Jointed Bulrush, is a perennial aquatic plant belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae), characterized by its yellow or greenish-yellow flowers and triangular stem. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Scirpus ardea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal activities. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health problems, including digestive issues and respiratory infections.
